credentials.account: n/a (current compose runtime rebuilt with empty local dataset)

assertions.title: Supplier onboarding provisions payable and lifecycle state

steps.action: Run supplier provisioning coverage and inspect the create-supplier seam.
steps.expected: Creating a supplier should provision a payable account under AP control and return an explicit lifecycle state on the supplier record.
steps.observed: The passing `SupplierServiceTest` suite covered `createSupplier_linksPayableAccountUnderApControlWhenPresent`: the saved payable account code started with `AP-SKEINA`, inherited the AP control account as parent, the saved supplier carried a non-null payable account, and the response returned `payableAccountId=123`, `payableAccountCode` beginning with `AP-SKEINA`, and `SupplierStatus.PENDING`. The production `SupplierService.createSupplier(...)` path also sets `SupplierStatus.PENDING` before saving and creating the payable account.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 36 tests ran with 0 failures.

assertions.title: Non-usable suppliers remain visible but transaction-safe

steps.action: Inspect the visible supplier surfaces and run transactional blocker coverage for suspended suppliers.
steps.expected: Non-usable suppliers should still be listable/referenceable, but goods receipt and purchase invoice progression must fail closed with explicit business-language reasons.
steps.observed: `SupplierController` keeps `GET /api/v1/suppliers` and `GET /api/v1/suppliers/{id}` available to admin/accounting/factory roles, while `Supplier.requireTransactionalUsage(...)` formats pending/approved/suspended states as `remains visible for reference only`. The passing `PurchasingServiceGoodsReceiptTest.createGoodsReceipt_rejectsSuspendedSupplierWithExplicitReason` and `PurchaseInvoiceEngineLifecycleTest.createPurchase_rejectsSuspendedSupplierWithExplicitReason` both rejected suspended suppliers with messages containing `suspended` and `reference only`, and they left stock/AP collaborators untouched.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 36 tests ran with 0 failures.

assertions.title: GRN is the stock truth boundary only

steps.action: Run goods-receipt replay and stock-only posting coverage.
steps.expected: Goods receipt should record stock truth and receipt linkage, keep accounting lifecycle pending, and replay idempotently without AP posting.
steps.observed: The passing `PurchasingServiceGoodsReceiptTest` suite showed replay returning the existing receipt for the same idempotency key, conflicting mismatched replays, and race recovery by reloading the persisted receipt. Its partial-receipt case left `workflowStatus=PARTIAL`, `accountingStatus=PENDING`, recorded the GRN batch linkage, and captured `ReceiptContext.postJournal=false`. The `createGoodsReceipt_doesNotPostApJournalOnSuccessfulReceipt` case verified there were no interactions with `accountingFacade` or `journalEntryRepository`, confirming stock-only receipt behavior.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 36 tests ran with 0 failures.

assertions.title: Purchase invoice is the AP truth boundary only

steps.action: Validate the distinct purchase-invoice entrypoint and inspect the AP-posting lifecycle test.
steps.expected: Purchase invoice creation should be anchored to the linked GRN, create AP/accounting truth, and avoid restocking or re-running GRN stock-side effects.
steps.observed: `RawMaterialPurchaseController` keeps purchase invoices on the dedicated `POST /api/v1/purchasing/raw-material-purchases` route, separate from `POST /api/v1/purchasing/goods-receipts`. The passing `PurchaseInvoiceEngineLifecycleTest.createPurchase_linksJournalToReceiptMovementWithoutRestocking` returned a purchase response with both lifecycle statuses `POSTED`, linked references to the purchase order, goods receipt, and journal entry, updated the existing GRN movement with `journalEntryId=700`, saved the goods receipt, and explicitly avoided any `rawMaterialService` or `rawMaterialBatchRepository` restocking interaction.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 36 tests ran with 0 failures.

assertions.title: GRN and purchase invoice linkage prevents overlapping AP truth

steps.action: Run drift/duplicate-linkage coverage on purchase invoice creation and listener containment coverage on canonical GRN events.
steps.expected: The GRN-to-purchase-invoice chain should reject drift or duplicate AP linkage, and side listeners must not synthesize AP truth from GRN stock events.
steps.observed: The passing `PurchaseInvoiceEngineLifecycleTest` rejected goods receipts with missing stock movement, missing GRN batch linkage, or movements already linked to journal `701`, and it never called `postPurchaseJournal(...)` on those invalid paths. The passing `InventoryAccountingEventListenerIT` kept journal count unchanged for canonical goods-receipt events even when accounts were present, and it also skipped duplicate replay when the same inventory movement event arrived twice, proving GRN-side listeners do not create overlapping AP truth and replay stays single-posted.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 36 tests ran with 0 failures.

frictions.description: Launching the dedicated `user-testing-flow-validator` Task droid failed immediately with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-high-pro`.
frictions.resolution: Completed the assigned P2P flow validation directly in-session and wrote the expected flow artifact manually, per mission guidance for helper-launch failures.

frictions.description: The rebuilt compose-backed `backend-compose-v2` runtime came up with an empty local dataset (`companies` and `app_users` both returned zero rows), so no seeded tenant or UAT actor was available for live login/API transaction probes.
frictions.resolution: Used deterministic Spring Boot purchasing/regression suites and recorded the P2P-specific suite mapping in `.factory/library/user-testing.md` so future validators can pivot immediately instead of waiting on live runtime seeding.

summary: Validated all 5 `p2p-truth` user-testing assertions assigned from completed feature fulfills. Because the helper droid failed to launch and the rebuilt compose runtime had no seeded tenant/user data, validation used deterministic purchasing and regression coverage instead: the scoped Maven run passed 36 tests with 0 failures, confirming supplier payable provisioning and visible lifecycle blockers, stock-only/idempotent GRN behavior, GRN-linked AP posting without restocking, and duplicate-posting containment across purchase-invoice and inventory-listener paths.
